task_completion.description: Verify the document includes the expected title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Office Emergency Response Procedures' typed at the top of the Word document.

task_completion.description: Verify the use of 'Legal Style' multi-level numbering.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the list formatting in the document. It must use the 1. / 1.1 / 1.1.1 numbering format for its hierarchy, rather than bullets, roman numerals, or letters.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the 3 main Level 1 sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the top-level numbered items (e.g., 1., 2., 3.): 'Fire Emergency', 'Medical Emergency', and 'Severe Weather'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the 8 Level 2 subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the second-level numbered items (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, etc.). They should include: 'Detection and Alarm', 'Evacuation', 'Post-Evacuation', 'Initial Response', 'First Aid', 'Documentation', 'Tornado Warning', and 'Flooding'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the Level 3 procedural items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the third-level numbered items (e.g., 1.1.1, 1.1.2, etc.) containing the specific procedural instructions like 'Activate nearest fire alarm pull station', 'Call 911 from a safe location', 'Use nearest marked exit', etc., nested under their respective Level 2 subsections.

task_completion.description: Verify the document is saved with the correct name in the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Word title bar showing 'Emergency_Response_Procedures.docx' or a File Explorer window/Save As dialog confirming the file is saved in the 'Documents' folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Microsoft Word 2010 GUI to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Microsoft Word 2010 application window open with the document being edited, rather than terminal windows executing scripts (like python-docx) to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with Word's formatting tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the agent using the Word ribbon, specifically the Paragraph group (Multi-level list button), to apply the Legal Style numbering.

integrity.description: Verify the document was constructed progressively.
integrity.visual_evidence: Earlier screenshots should show partial completion of the document or list structure, indicating the agent typed or constructed the content within the Word interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Environment: Microsoft Word 2010. Output Path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Emergency_Response_Procedures.docx. Expected Title: 'Office Emergency Response Procedures'. The document must contain a 3-level list using 1. / 1.1 / 1.1.1 format. Level 1 items (3): Fire Emergency, Medical Emergency, Severe Weather. Level 2 items (8): Detection and Alarm, Evacuation, Post-Evacuation, Initial Response, First Aid, Documentation, Tornado Warning, Flooding. Level 3 items (17): Activate nearest fire alarm pull station, Call 911 from a safe location, Use nearest marked exit, Do not use elevators, Proceed to designated assembly point, Account for all personnel, Report missing persons to emergency responders, Call 911 immediately for life-threatening conditions, Do not move the injured person unless in immediate danger, Administer CPR if trained and certified, Use AED if available and trained, Complete incident report within 24 hours, Submit report to Human Resources, Move to interior rooms on lowest floor, Stay away from windows and exterior walls, Move equipment to higher ground if time permits, Evacuate building if directed by management.
